title: dianthus gracilis subsp. gracilis
intro: Dianthus gracilis subsp. gracilis, also known as Fringed Pink, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dianthus gracilis subsp. gracilis include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or dried flower extract, which are used in herbal remedies to harness its medicinal properties.
